author: Robert Carr
description: Fresh table salsa with whole peeled tomatoes, fire-roasted tomatoes, jalapeno, white onion, garlic, cilantro, lime, cumin, salt, and a short low-speed pulse technique that keeps it lightly chunky.
ingredients: 1 28-ounce can whole peeled tomatoes, drained with 1/2 cup juice reserved
1/2 cup cilantro leaves and tender stems
1 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, or 1/2 teaspoon Morton kosher salt
instructions: Drain 1 28-ounce can whole peeled tomatoes, reserving 1/2 cup of the juice, and drain 1 14.5-ounce can fire-roasted tomatoes.

Add 1 jalapeno, 1/3 cup roughly chopped white onion, 1 roughly chopped garlic clove, 1/2 cup cilantro, 2 tablespoons fresh lime juice, 1 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, 1/2 teaspoon cumin, 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, and 1/2 teaspoon granulated sugar if using to a blender.
Add about half of the drained tomatoes over the jalapeno, onion, garlic, and cilantro.
Pulse on low speed 2 to 4 times, just until the jalapeno, onion, garlic, and cilantro are chopped.
Add the remaining drained tomatoes and pulse on low speed 3 to 6 more times, stopping while the salsa still has small tomato pieces and a loose table-salsa texture.
If the salsa is thicker than you like, stir in reserved tomato juice 1 tablespoon at a time with a rubber spatula instead of blending longer.
Transfer the salsa to a bowl, taste, and adjust only if needed with a small pinch of kosher salt, a few drops of lime juice, or a pinch of sugar.
Cover and rest for 45 minutes before serving so the canned tomato, onion, cilantro, lime, and cumin flavors come together.
notes: Whole peeled tomatoes give a better blended texture than using only diced tomatoes. The fire-roasted tomatoes add charred depth without making the salsa taste fully cooked.
This salsa needs restraint in a powerful blender. If using a Vitamix 5200, pulse on Variable 2 or 3 in very short bursts instead of blending continuously.
Do not fix thickness by blending longer. Stir in reserved tomato juice instead so the salsa stays fresh and lightly chunky.
For a milder salsa, remove the jalapeno seeds. For a hotter salsa, use 2 jalapenos or add 1 serrano.
The sugar is optional and should only be used if the tomatoes taste flat or metallic.
This salsa works especially well with chips, tacos, eggs, rice bowls, grilled meats, and simple weeknight meals.
title: Fresh Table Salsa
